title: Distant Horizon quest template
description: A seed-agnostic quest template. Slots bind against the generated world at world-gen or trigger time; constraints share one AST vocabulary across slots, eligibility (checked at offer/accept), conduct (latched invariants while active), completion (turn-in check), and fail_when (author-listed failure conditions). See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-16-quests-design.md. TODO(dialogue): dialogue beyond the single 'flavor' string is not yet modeled — dialogue points should become arrays of variant phrasings, each with a speaker (offering broker by default; rumor-bearers and passenger items are also speakers). TODO(events): events do not exist yet; when they do they become an additional trigger source posting these templates (likely an 'event' acquisition mode). Name pools: character and ship slots are generated principals whose names come from the server/names/ pools; character slots expose dotted interpolation forms ${x.given}/${x.family}/${x.short}/${x.title} and pronouns ${x.ey}/${x.em}/${x.eir}/${x.eirs}/${x.emself} (capitalized variants Ey/Em/Eir/Eirs/Emself; authored in ey/em/eir, rendered she/he for gendered-culture characters), ship slots expose ${x.role} — see docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-17-quest-principals-and-name-pools-design.md. TODO(trigger-resolution): triggers currently fire immediately and always; they should eventually support chance (probabilistic follow-up), delay_s (a fuse before the child posts), and weighted one-of-N selection (a trigger that picks one child from several options by weight).

name.description: Display name. ${slot} interpolations are substituted with the bound entity's display name at offer time (e.g. "Water for ${dest}").

flavor.description: Offer prose. ${slot} interpolations are substituted with bound display names, as in name.

acquisition.description: broker: offered at a faction broker console — specifically the broker bound by the slot named 'offer_broker', which broker-acquisition quests must declare. rumor: ambient, no contract, no penalty for ignoring. triggered: only reachable via another quest's triggers.

slots.description: Named entities the quest needs from the generated world. ${slotName} interpolates a bound slot anywhere a constraint value appears. If a slot cannot bind, the quest does not exist this run.

eligibility.description: Snapshot check at offer/accept time (rep gates, ship fit, crew).

conduct.description: Latched invariants monitored while the quest is active. A violation latches; default effect is quest failure unless the predicate carries on_violation.

completion.description: Turn-in check, evaluated at the moment of hand-over.

fail_when.description: Author-listed failure conditions. The engine additionally always enforces implicit fails: destination gone, quest item destroyed or sold, quest passenger dead.

deadline_s.description: Seconds from acceptance. Absent = undated (the opportunity flavor).

effects.description: World effects by outcome — the third consequence kind besides rewards (crew) and triggers (quests). Applied by the engine when the outcome resolves; once a world simulation exists, effects become discrete story-beat inputs to it, not overrides.

world_effect.description: Vocabulary of world effects; grows as content demands.

kind.description: station/broker/faction/commodity slots BIND existing world entities. character/ship slots are GENERATED: the resolver conjures a principal satisfying the constraints, deterministically from the quest-instance seed, with names drawn from the tag-matched pools in server/names/ (authority: schemas/names.schema.json). Generated slots never veto a quest's existence; build-time coverage tests guarantee non-empty pools.

from.description: Inherit a resolved binding from the triggering parent quest, e.g. parent.dest or parent.item.remains. Only valid in quests with acquisition: triggered (test-enforced).

constraint.description: Mongo-style constraint AST. Sibling keys are implicit AND; any/all/not give explicit logic. Leaf predicates are the enumerated vocabulary below. String values may be literals or ${slot} interpolations.

built_by.description: The station's builder — a manufacturer id (lore.md: yards build stations too; e.g. built_by: apogee_grand matches any Apogee Grand hotel station in the seed).

race_is.description: Generation leaf (character slots): human | selkie | grafter | senti.

wealth_is.description: Generation leaf (character slots): low | mid | high.

gender_is.description: Generation leaf (character slots): female | male | neutral. Unpinned, the resolver rolls 47.5/47.5/5.

role_is.description: Generation leaf (ship slots): packet | hauler | liner | gunship | tug | yacht (authority: names.schema.json; shared with future hull auto-categorization).

deliver_commodity.description: Hand over open-market commodity units. 'to' names the receiving broker slot; absent = offload at the current dock.

deliver_item.description: Hand over a quest item (or disembark a passenger). 'to' names the receiving broker slot; absent = offload/disembark at the current dock.

while_aboard.description: Item id scoping the invariant to while that item is aboard. Absent = active for the quest's whole life.

violation.description: Softens a conduct violation from quest-failure to a reward penalty.

ref_string.description: A literal id or a ${slot} interpolation.

name.description: Display name; ${slot} interpolations substitute as everywhere. Absent = the principal's full display name when principal is set, else the resolver generates one from the seed.

principal.description: Name of a character slot this item embodies — the berth passenger IS that character (test-enforced to reference a character slot).

space.description: hold: occupies cargo units. berth: a passenger — named, mortal, otherwise a special more annoying kind of cargo.

pickup_at.description: Station slot ref where the item is injected when the crew docks. Absent = injected at acceptance.

item.description: Quest items are namespaced by quest id (wire id: <questId>.<itemId>) and are not sellable on the open market.

target.description: A faction or manufacturer id (part unlocks and discounts are modeled as rep bumps).

meta_unlock.description: Rare. The only reward that outlives the run (e.g. senti_start).

mode.description: first_refusal: offered privately to the completing crew for window_s, then 'then' happens. open: posted at an appropriate broker, first-come.
